risk_profile.max_loss: put strike minus premium received (stock to zero)

entry_conditions: uptrend or sideways price action confirmed
clear area of support identified at or below strike
open interest >= 100 (prefer 500+)
underlying ADV >= 500k shares
sell OTM strike below current price
exit_rules: let expire worthless if underlying holds above strike
buy back early if 50-80% of max profit achieved
buy back immediately if underlying breaches stop-loss level
adjustment_rules: roll down and out (lower strike, later expiry) if short strike threatened
failure_modes: gap-down through strike on news/macro event
IV spike inflates mark-to-market loss before expiry
assignment forces buying falling stock at strike

evidence.finding_summary: Standard income strategy; Cohen classifies as intermediate proficiency. Return on risk example: 4.38% with 12.43% cushion from breakeven.
evidence.notes: Cohen: only sell on stocks you would be willing to own at the strike price. Position strike around strong support within a rising trend.

entry_conditions: bullish or neutral directional outlook
short strike at or below key support level
elevated IV preferred for higher credit
both strikes OTM at entry
exit_rules: let expire worthless if underlying holds above short strike
close early at 50% of max profit to reduce gamma risk
close or roll if underlying trades through short strike with DTE remaining
adjustment_rules: roll spread down and out if short strike breached (lower strikes, later expiry)
close losing leg first, then reopen at improved strike
failure_modes: underlying breaks through short put strike
IV spike widens mark-to-market loss
entering into earnings or macro event causes gap risk
early assignment on short put leg if deep ITM near ex-dividend

evidence.finding_summary: DERA (2024): vertical put spreads comprise 34% of ETP complex options volume — most common complex structure in ETF universe. Cohen: classified as income strategy, intermediate proficiency.
evidence.notes: Cohen formula: max_loss = spread_width - net_credit; breakeven = short_strike - net_credit.

entry_conditions: bearish or neutral directional outlook
short strike at or above key resistance level
elevated IV preferred
both strikes OTM at entry
downtrend or rangebound price action confirmed
exit_rules: let expire worthless if underlying holds below short strike
close early at 50% of max profit
close or roll if underlying rallies through short strike
adjustment_rules: roll up and out (higher strikes, later expiry) if short call strike breached
close full position and accept loss if trend is strongly bullish
failure_modes: underlying rallies through short call strike
IV spike after entry worsens position
catalyst event (earnings, FOMC) causes gap through short strike
early assignment on short call if deep ITM

evidence.finding_summary: DERA (2024): vertical call spreads are the most common complex stock option structure (36% of stock complex volume). Call spread volume is positively predicted by recent positive underlying returns — directional alignment is essential.
evidence.notes: Cohen: breakeven = short_call_strike + net_credit; max_loss = spread_width - net_credit.

greeks.gamma: negative (losses accelerate with large moves in either direction)

risk_profile.max_profit: total net credit from both spreads
risk_profile.max_loss: width of wider spread minus total net credit (one side only)

entry_conditions: neutral/rangebound market outlook
IV rank elevated vs historical (prefer IV rank > 30%)
short strikes outside expected 1-standard-deviation move
both short strikes OTM at entry
credit >= 30-33% of spread width
no major catalyst events within DTE window
exit_rules: close at 50% of max profit to eliminate gamma risk
close full position if net loss exceeds 2x initial credit received
manage individual threatened wing before full position close
adjustment_rules: roll threatened wing further OTM and/or further out in time
if both wings threatened (vol spike), close entire position — do not fight large directional move
never widen a losing spread — max loss must not increase on adjustment
failure_modes: sharp directional move through either short strike
IV spike after entry (negative vega)
overnight gap risk from macro or earnings event
pin risk at short strikes near expiration

evidence.finding_summary: DERA (2024): iron condors are predominantly sold in ETP options. Cohen: intermediate proficiency, defined-risk version of short straddle. Strictly preferred over short strangle from capital-preservation standpoint.
evidence.notes: Cohen lower breakeven = short_put_strike - net_credit; upper breakeven = short_call_strike + net_credit. Iron condor = bull put spread + bear call spread on same underlying/expiry.

risk_profile.max_profit: total premium received from both legs
risk_profile.max_loss: unlimited on upside; downside limited by underlying going to zero

entry_conditions: neutral outlook; underlying expected to remain in range
high IV environment — IV must be elevated vs history
short strikes outside expected 1-standard-deviation move
underlying not in strong trend (ADX < 25 preferred)
exit_rules: close at 50% of max profit
close or roll if either short strike is approached
hard stop if loss exceeds 2x credit received
adjustment_rules: roll threatened leg further OTM and/or out in time
convert to iron condor by adding long wings if IV spikes unexpectedly
failure_modes: large directional move through either short strike — undefined loss
IV spike inflates both short legs simultaneously
margin call risk due to undefined risk profile
entering in low IV — premium insufficient to justify undefined risk

evidence.finding_summary: Cohen: advanced proficiency due to unlimited risk. DERA (2024): strangles are predominantly sold in ETP options but represent only ~6% of complex volume. Iron condor strictly preferred from risk perspective.
evidence.notes: Upper breakeven = call_strike + total_premium; lower breakeven = put_strike - total_premium. Cohen rates as advanced due to uncapped risk.

risk_profile.max_loss: unlimited upside; strike minus premium to downside

entry_conditions: strongly neutral outlook
very high IV environment
underlying at or near ATM strike
exit_rules: close at 50% of max profit
close immediately on large directional move
adjustment_rules: convert to strangle by moving strikes OTM
add wings to convert to iron butterfly (defined risk)
failure_modes: any large directional move — most painful options position on a gap
ATM strikes mean delta shifts rapidly in either direction
IV spike doubles pain (both legs expand)

evidence.finding_summary: Cohen: advanced proficiency. Higher premium than strangle but much tighter profit zone. ATM gamma is highest — position most sensitive to moves near strike.
evidence.notes: Upper breakeven = ATM_strike + total_premium; lower breakeven = ATM_strike - total_premium. Cohen rates as advanced/expert.

greeks.theta: positive (front month decays faster than back month)
greeks.vega: positive (long vega — benefits from IV increase, unlike most income strategies)

risk_profile.max_profit: when underlying is at strike at front-month expiry (max time value differential)

entry_conditions: mildly bullish or neutral outlook
low IV environment (long vega benefits from subsequent IV expansion)
underlying near strike at entry
exit_rules: close near front-month expiry
sell back month if position achieves profit target
adjustment_rules: roll front-month short leg to next expiry if underlying has not moved
failure_modes: large move away from strike collapses time-value differential
IV crush after entry reduces back-month value

evidence.finding_summary: DERA (2024): calendar spreads account for 22% of stock complex volume and 12% of ETP complex volume. Primary use is rolling expiring positions, not harvesting payoff. Near leg of calendar predominantly closes existing positions; far leg opens new ones.
evidence.notes: Unique among income strategies: positive vega. Cohen: intermediate proficiency.

risk_profile.max_profit: variable — depends on both strike and expiry movement

entry_conditions: bullish outlook with expectation underlying stays below short call strike near-term
IV term structure in contango (near-term IV lower than far-term)
exit_rules: roll short leg repeatedly at expiry
close both legs if underlying rallies above short strike
adjustment_rules: roll short leg up and out if underlying approaches short strike
failure_modes: underlying rallies sharply through short strike
IV collapse reduces back-month value

evidence.finding_summary: DERA (2024): diagonal spreads account for part of the 57% spread complex volume in ETPs. Volume spikes when underlying makes large move — primarily used to roll strikes away from spot.
evidence.notes: Cohen: intermediate proficiency. DERA: diagonal near leg closes existing position; far leg opens new position.

source_title: Simple Roles for Complex Options (DERA Working Paper)

evidence.finding_summary: Vertical spreads (call and put combined) account for 34% of ETP complex options volume; all spread types (vertical, calendar, diagonal, vertical ratio, roll) account for 57% of ETP complex volume. Straddles/strangles/butterflies/condors combined = max 6% of ETP complex volume. ETPs have higher complex ratio (36%) than stocks (24%). HYG complex ratio = 65%, SLV = 14% (lowest).
evidence.notes: Sample: top 30 stock + 30 ETP options classes by volume. Data: OPRA intraday + OptionMetrics. OTM-OTM is most common vertical configuration; ITM-ITM is least common.

model_features: etp_vs_stock_flag (ETPs have structurally higher complex volume)

source_title: Simple Roles for Complex Options (DERA Working Paper)
adjustment_rules: primary trigger for rolling is moneyness change — short strike approaching ITM — not DTE alone
call spread volume increases with positive underlying returns (short call strike threatened)
put spread volume increases with negative underlying returns (short put strike threatened)
near-expiry calendar/diagonal legs predominantly close existing positions; far legs open new ones
calendar/diagonal volume spikes on expiration week (35% near leg expiring same Friday on Monday, 50% by Friday)
failure_modes: rolling driven by DTE alone (without moneyness trigger) does not reflect empirical market behavior
rolling a losing position to wider strikes increases max loss — never widen a spread on adjustment

evidence.finding_summary: Call spread volume is positively and put spread volume is negatively related to recent underlying returns (8 predictive regressions, stock and ETP samples, class and date fixed effects). ITM leg closes, OTM leg opens in vertical adjustments. Calendar near leg closes, far leg opens. This mechanism — not payoff harvesting — is the primary driver of complex options volume.
evidence.notes: Regression results statistically significant at class and date fixed effects level. Pattern consistent across stocks and ETPs.

model_features: delta_of_short_leg (primary roll trigger: exceeds threshold e.g. 0.40)
underlying_5d_return (predictive of which spread type is rolled)
days_to_expiry_near_leg (expiry week spikes in calendar/diagonal rolls)

source_title: Simple Roles for Complex Options (DERA Working Paper)
strategy_name: ETP Put Skew and Volatility Selling

greeks.vega: negative (vol sellers dominate strangles, iron condors, iron butterflies in ETPs)

evidence.finding_summary: ETP options skew toward puts (put volume > call volume for IWM, HYG, SPY, QQQ in both simple and complex trades) — consistent with investors using ETP options to hedge downside. In ETP volatility trades: strangles, iron condors, and iron butterflies are predominantly sold (negative vega initiation); straddles are more often bought (positive vega). Short premium on ETF puts sells into natural structural demand from hedgers.
evidence.notes: Stock options skew toward calls (levered upside bets). ETP options skew toward puts (downside hedging). This structural asymmetry makes ETP put premium selling (ShortPutSpread, CSP) a flow-consistent strategy.

model_features: skew_metric (OTM put IV minus ATM IV)

source_title: Simple Roles for Complex Options (DERA Working Paper)
entry_conditions: always use multi-leg complex orders — never leg into spreads separately
failure_modes: legging into spreads separately can cost up to 83% more in transaction costs
S-LOB execution is costliest electronic complex protocol
C-LOB is middle ground; C-PIAs provide best price improvement

evidence.finding_summary: Buying vertical spreads through complex orders rather than two single-leg trades saves up to 83% on transaction costs (Li, Musto and Pearson, 2023). C-PIAs provide best price improvement among electronic protocols. C-LOB is more cost-effective than S-LOB for complex orders.
evidence.notes: This applies directly to bot execution: multi-leg limit orders are required, never leg separately.

source_title: The U.S. Treasury Yield Curve: 1961 to the Present (Fed FEDS 2006-28)
strategy_name: Yield Curve Slope as Macro Regime Filter
entry_conditions: yield curve slope (10y minus 2y) > 0: normal/steepening — standard premium selling allowed
yield curve slope near zero or flattening: elevated macro risk — tighten IV and delta requirements
yield curve inverted (2y > 10y, T10Y2Y < 0): recession risk elevated — maximum selectivity, reduce position sizing
exit_rules: rapid curve inversion (flattening > 50bps in 30 days) is a macro stress signal — reduce exposure
failure_modes: ignoring curve inversion as a regime signal — yield curve inversion preceded every US recession since 1961
treating yield curve data as static — curve updates daily

evidence.finding_summary: GSW model produces daily Treasury yield curve estimates from 1961 to present using Nelson-Siegel-Svensson smoothing. Data available via Federal Reserve website (updated daily) and FRED. Key FRED tickers: T10Y2Y (10y-2y spread), DGS2, DGS5, DGS10, DGS3MO, T10Y3M.
evidence.notes: Yield curve inversion has preceded every US recession since 1961. Bot already consumes T10Y2Y via FRED for credit regime signal. No new data integration required — methodology paper only.

model_features: T10Y2Y (10y minus 2y spread — primary slope metric)
T10Y3M (10y minus 3m spread — alternative inversion signal)

source_title: The U.S. Treasury Yield Curve: 1961 to the Present (Fed FEDS 2006-28)
strategy_name: Yield Curve Forward Rate as Macro Stress Signal
entry_conditions: negative term premium (investors accepting lower yield for safety) — risk-off macro regime
near-term forward rates above long-term forward rates — inversion signal
failure_modes: relying solely on on-the-run Treasury yields (GSW uses off-the-run to avoid liquidity premium distortion)

evidence.finding_summary: GSW model provides instantaneous forward rates and n-by-m forward rates for any maturity. Term premium embedded in yield curve: when negative, investors prefer safety — risk-off signal. Off-the-run yield curve used to avoid on-the-run liquidity premium distortion.
evidence.notes: Daily frequency. Forward rates available from the Fed website. Advanced macro signal — term premium extraction requires GSW model output not currently pulled by bot.

source_title: Technical Analysis for Options Trading (Fidelity Investments Webinar)
strategy_name: Bollinger Band Squeeze as Regime Filter
entry_conditions: wide Bollinger Bands (expanded bandwidth) = elevated vol = favorable for short premium entry
narrow Bollinger Bands (squeeze = bandwidth at multi-week low) = do NOT enter iron condor or short strangle — large move imminent
exit_rules: BB expansion after position entry signals vol risk to short positions — consider early close
failure_modes: entering short-premium position during BB squeeze — underlying about to break out
BB width is backward-looking — does not predict direction of breakout, only imminence

evidence.finding_summary: Fidelity guide identifies Bollinger Bands as one of five key TA concepts for options traders. Squeeze (narrow bands) precedes large moves — adverse for short-premium (negative vega) strategies. Wide bands indicate elevated recent realized vol — favorable environment for premium selling.
evidence.notes: Standard: 20-period SMA ± 2 standard deviations. Bandwidth = (upper band - lower band) / middle band.

source_title: Technical Analysis for Options Trading (Fidelity Investments Webinar)
entry_conditions: uptrend (higher highs + higher lows): favor bullish strategies — CSP, ShortPutSpread; place short put strike at or below support
downtrend (lower highs + lower lows): favor bearish strategies — ShortCallSpread; place short call strike at or above resistance
sideways trend: favor neutral strategies — IronCondor, ShortStrangle; short strikes outside S/R boundaries
moving average crossover (e.g. 50-day crosses above 200-day) confirms trend regime shift
exit_rules: adverse trend change post-entry is a signal for early exit or adjustment
underlying breaching moving average support = risk trigger for bullish short-put positions
adjustment_rules: if support breaks post-entry on ShortPutSpread, roll short leg to new support level
failure_modes: entering a trend-based strategy in a rangebound market (ADX < 20) — false signals
using only one TA indicator without confirmation from trend context
TA patterns are never mechanically precise — require contextual interpretation

evidence.finding_summary: Fidelity guide: trend direction defines strategy bias; support/resistance defines strike placement. Technical analysis is the key interface between price action and options strike selection. Key rule: trend is assumed to continue rather than reverse.
evidence.notes: Five core concepts: trends, support/resistance, moving averages, breakouts, Bollinger Bands. Strike selection is primary application for options traders.

source_title: 25 Proven Strategies for Trading Options on CME Group Futures

risk_profile.max_loss: substantial as market falls (futures to zero)

entry_conditions: bullish on futures market
uncertain about volatility
exit_rules: buy back if market falls through stop
let expire worthless if market remains above strike
failure_modes: futures market drops sharply through strike
no early assignment risk for American-style futures options vs equity options

evidence.finding_summary: CME booklet: strategy #10. Decay characteristics: positive (time decay helps). Pattern evolution diagrams show position at 4 months, 1 month, expiry.
evidence.notes: Futures options — no early assignment risk (European-style settlement). Mechanically similar to equity short put but different margin treatment. Synthetics: long call A + short call A (synthetic long futures) is equivalent.

source_title: 25 Proven Strategies for Trading Options on CME Group Futures
entry_conditions: neutral market outlook
uncertain about direction but expecting range-bound behavior
high IV environment
exit_rules: buy back both legs if either strike is breached
let expire worthless if market remains within range
failure_modes: large directional move in either direction
IV spike after entry

evidence.finding_summary: CME booklet: strategy #20. Wider profit zone than short straddle but less premium. Pattern evolution shows position value decaying toward max profit at expiry if underlying remains between strikes.
evidence.notes: Futures version: no early assignment, cash-settled. Mechanically identical to equity short strangle in concept. Breakeven: upper = call_strike + total_premium; lower = put_strike - total_premium.

source_title: 25 Proven Strategies for Trading Options on CME Group Futures

greeks.gamma: positive near lower strike, negative near higher

risk_profile.max_profit: spread width minus net cost (call debit) or net credit (put credit)
risk_profile.max_loss: net debit (call debit spread) or spread width minus credit (put credit spread)

entry_conditions: bullish on futures market
exit_rules: let expire if profitable at expiry
close early if significant profit achieved
failure_modes: market fails to rally above lower strike — full debit lost

evidence.finding_summary: CME booklet: strategy #11. Can be implemented as call debit spread (long lower call, short higher call) or put credit spread (short higher put, long lower put). Pattern evolution shows profit zone development as expiry approaches.
evidence.notes: CME context is futures options. Directly analogous to equity bull call spread / bull put spread.
